title: Certificates of Deposit (CDs)
subtitle: One of the Safest Ways to Grow Your Money
content:
What is a CD?

  • A special type of savings account
  • You agree to leave money for a fixed period (the term)
  • Bank pays you a higher interest rate than regular savings
  • The catch: You can't withdraw early without a penalty
Think of it like a promise:
You guarantee not to touch the money → Bank pays you more interest.

title: Key CD Terms
subtitle: Know the Vocabulary
content:
PrincipalAmount you deposit
TermLength of time (3 months to 5+ years)
APYAnnual Percentage Yield — effective rate including compounding
Early Withdrawal PenaltyFee for breaking the agreement

title: CD Math — Worked Example
subtitle: $2,000 CD at 4.5% APY, Compounded Monthly, 2 Years
content:
A = P × (1 + r/n)n×t
P = $2,000r = 0.045
n = 12 (monthly)t = 2 years

Step by step:
  1. 0.045 ÷ 12 = 0.00375
  2. 1 + 0.00375 = 1.00375
  3. 1.0037524 ≈ 1.09381
  4. $2,000 × 1.09381 = $2,187.62
You earned $187.62 by doing absolutely nothing.

title: Why Choose a CD?
subtitle: Pros and Cons
content:
✅ Pros

  • Higher rates than savings accounts
  • Predictable — rate locked in at opening
  • Safe — FDIC insured up to $250,000

❌ Cons

  • Low liquidity — money is locked up
  • Early withdrawal penalty eats into earnings
  • Rate risk — stuck if rates go up after you open

title: The CD Ladder Strategy
subtitle: Best of Both Worlds
content:
How It Works

Split your money across multiple CDs with different terms:

AmountTerm
$1,0001-year CD
$1,0002-year CD
$1,0003-year CD
$1,0004-year CD
$1,0005-year CD
Every year, one CD matures → Reinvest at current rates or use if needed.
Higher long-term rates + regular access to money.

title: The Numbers Don't Lie
subtitle: $5,000 Over 5 Years — Comparison
content:
Savings Account

0.5% interest

~$126

earned

1-Year CD

4.0% compounded

~$1,083

earned

5-Year CD

4.75% compounded

~$1,311

earned

The CD earns 10× more than the savings account.

title: Real-World Takeaway + Assignment
subtitle: When to Use CDs
content:
Use CDs when you know you won't need the money:

  • 🚗 Saving for a car in 2 years? → CD
  • 🏠 Down payment over 5 years? → CD Ladder
  • 🚨 Emergency fund? → Regular savings (don't lock it up)

📝 Assignment

  1. Calculate maturity value of 3 CDs with different terms & rates
  2. Design a CD ladder strategy for $10,000
  3. Explain your reasoning
  4. Show all your work!
